info.title: AXIAM API
info.description: Access eXtended Identity and Authorization Management — REST API

get.description:
Returns the OpenID Provider metadata per OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0.
The issuer URL is taken from `AuthConfig::oauth2_issuer_url` when set,
falling back to `AuthConfig::jwt_issuer` otherwise.

Returns audit entries for unauthenticated/system requests (nil tenant_id).
Requires authentication — only logged-in users can query system audit logs.

post.description:
Authenticate a device via its client certificate (mTLS).
The certificate must be bound to a service account.

403.description: Certificate not bound to a service account

post.description:
Consumes the state row (single-use), runs the verified OIDC flow from
plan 04-02 (nonce from DB — not caller-supplied), provisions or links the
user, and returns Set-Cookie response (no token in body).

Generates a server-side state+nonce pair, persists it in
`federation_login_state` (10-min TTL), and returns the IdP authorization
URL.  No JWT required — this is the first step of first-time SSO (D-22).
Confirm MFA enrollment and complete login using a setup token.

401.description: Invalid or expired setup token / TOTP code

post.description:
Start MFA enrollment using a setup token (issued during login when
MFA is enforced but not yet configured).

Creates a new email verification token and enqueues it for async
delivery via the mail queue (D-14).  Always returns `{"sent": true}`
to prevent email enumeration (D-15) — regardless of whether the email
exists, is already verified, or has hit a rate limit.

Initiates a password reset by enqueuing an `OutboundMailMessage` to the
async mail queue.  Always returns `{"sent": true}` to prevent email
enumeration (D-15) — regardless of whether the email exists, the user is
federated, delivery later succeeds, or the rate limit is exceeded.

Confirms a password reset using a one-time token and a new
password. The token is consumed atomically.

Returns `{"reset": true}` on success, or 400 with policy
violations if the new password is too weak.

400.description: Invalid token or password policy violation

post.description:
Verifies a user's email using a one-time token. The token is
consumed atomically — replaying the same token returns an error.

Complete a WebAuthn passkey authentication ceremony.  On success
a session is created and access/refresh tokens are issued.

Begin a WebAuthn passkey authentication ceremony.  Requires a
valid MFA challenge token (obtained from the login flow).
Complete a WebAuthn passkey registration ceremony.

Begin a WebAuthn passkey registration ceremony for the
authenticated user.

Evaluate a single authorization check for the authenticated caller (or, if
the caller holds `authz:check_as`, for an arbitrary subject).

`tenant_id` is always taken from the JWT — it cannot be supplied in the body.

403.description: Forbidden — caller lacks authz:check_as for subject_id override

post.description:
Evaluate an ordered list of authorization checks.  Returns results in the
same order and length as the input `checks` array.

If **any** check in the batch includes a `subject_id`, the caller must hold
`authz:check_as` — the permission is verified once up front (not per item).

403.description: Forbidden — caller lacks authz:check_as for subject_id override

items.description:
A tenant-level certificate for users, services, or IoT devices.

Certificates are signed by the organization's CA. The private key is
returned once on generation and never stored by AXIAM.

issuer_ca_id.description: The CA certificate that signed this certificate.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata (e.g., device serial, user ID binding).

tenant_id.description: The tenant this certificate belongs to.

post.description:
Builds the authorization URL for the external OIDC provider. The caller
is responsible for generating and storing `state` and `nonce` values
for CSRF and replay protection.

TODO(T19.9): This endpoint currently requires authentication, making it
usable only for account-linking (already-authenticated users). Add
separate unauthenticated federation login endpoints that complete the
external flow and return access/refresh tokens for first-time login.

Handles the callback from the external OIDC provider after user
authentication. Exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
validates the ID token, and provisions or links the local user.

items.description:
A group of users that can access resources based on their roles
and permissions. Groups simplify role management by allowing roles
to be assigned to a group rather than individual users.
Public-safe user representation (no password_hash, no mfa_secret).

email_verified.description:
True when the user's email address has been verified
(`email_verified_at` is set). Exposed for the admin UI / profile.

failed_login_attempts.description: Number of consecutive failed login attempts.

is_locked.description: Whether the account is currently locked (locked_until is in the future).

locked_until.description: Timestamp until which the account is locked, if any.

items.description:
An organization groups multiple tenants under a single administrative entity.

Organizations represent companies, departments, or business units.
CA certificates are registered at the organization level, enabling
a hierarchical trust model across all tenants.

A CA (Certificate Authority) certificate at the organization level.

CA certificates are the root of trust for all tenant certificates
within the organization. Private keys for signing CAs are encrypted
with AES-256-GCM and stored separately; non-signing CAs only store
the public certificate.

organization_id.description: The organization this CA belongs to.

subject.description: The certificate subject (e.g., `CN=ACME Corp Root CA`).

404.description: No email configuration set for this organization

items.description:
A tenant is an isolated context within an organization.

Each tenant has its own set of users, roles, permissions, resources,
certificates, and configuration. Tenants can represent environments
(dev/staging/prod) or separate business contexts.

organization_id.description: The organization this tenant belongs to.

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier within the organization (e.g., `production`).

status.description: Lifecycle status. New tenants default to [`TenantStatus::Active`].

items.description: An OpenPGP key stored by AXIAM.

parent_id.description: Parent resource ID for hierarchical organization. `None` for root resources.

is_global.description: Global roles grant permissions across all resources.

get.description: Lists the groups directly assigned this role.

parameters.name: resource_id
parameters.description:
If provided, only removes the assignment for this specific resource.
If omitted, removes the global (unscoped) assignment.

get.description:
Lists the users directly assigned this role (the inverse of
`GET /users/{id}/roles`). Used by the role detail page's members panel.

parameters.name: resource_id
parameters.description:
If provided, only removes the assignment for this specific resource.
If omitted, removes the global (unscoped) assignment.

get.description:
Returns the effective (merged) security settings for the
authenticated user's tenant. Org baseline + tenant overrides.

put.description:
Set tenant-level overrides. Only fields that are **more restrictive**
than the org baseline are accepted. Omit a field (set to `null`) to
inherit from the org.

404.description: No email configuration override set for this tenant

200.description: Tenant email configuration override updated (secrets omitted)

items.description: Public-safe user representation (no password_hash, no mfa_secret).

email_verified.description:
True when the user's email address has been verified
(`email_verified_at` is set). Exposed for the admin UI / profile.

failed_login_attempts.description: Number of consecutive failed login attempts.

is_locked.description: Whether the account is currently locked (locked_until is in the future).

locked_until.description: Timestamp until which the account is locked, if any.

post.description:
Creates a user and atomically records a `terms_of_service` consent row
(REQ-8 / Art. 7 proof of consent).  IP address and User-Agent are
captured for the consent record.

get.description: List all MFA methods registered for a user.

403.description: Cannot view another user's MFA methods

delete.description:
Remove a specific MFA method. Returns 400 if it is the last
method and MFA is enabled.

403.description: Cannot delete another user's MFA methods

post.description:
Reset MFA for a user — disables MFA, clears the secret, and
revokes all existing sessions. Requires admin access (caller must
be in the same tenant).

Resets a locked user account: clears `locked_until`, resets
`failed_login_attempts` to 0, and sets status back to `Active`.

items.description: Webhook response — omits the shared secret.

get.description:
The user must be authenticated (redirected to login first if not).
On success, redirects to `redirect_uri?code=...&state=...`.
On error, redirects with `?error=...&error_description=...&state=...`.

post.description:
Accepts form-encoded body. Returns an `IntrospectionResponse` with
`active: true/false` and optional metadata.

get.description:
Returns the public signing keys used by the authorization server
so that relying parties can verify JWTs without sharing a secret.

post.description:
Accepts form-encoded body. Always returns 200 per the spec — invalid
tokens are silently ignored.

Accepts form-encoded body per RFC 6749. Supports authorization_code,
client_credentials, and refresh_token grant types. The `tenant_id` is
passed as a query parameter since the token endpoint is unauthenticated
(the client is authenticating itself here).

get.description:
Returns claims about the authenticated user. Requires a valid
Bearer access token. Email and username are included based on
the scopes present in the access token.

ApiProviderConfig.description:
API-based provider configuration (SendGrid, Postmark, Resend, Brevo).

`api_key` follows the same write-only + omit-preserving contract as
[`SmtpConfig::password`] (D-01/D-02).

api_url.description: Override base URL (useful for testing / self-hosted instances).

BatchCheckAccessBody.description: Body for a batch authorization check.

checks.description: Ordered list of checks to evaluate.

BatchCheckAccessResponse.description: Result of a batch authorization check.

results.description: Ordered results — same length and order as the input `checks`.

BindCertificate.description: Request to bind a certificate to a service account.

CaCertificate.description:
A CA (Certificate Authority) certificate at the organization level.

CA certificates are the root of trust for all tenant certificates
within the organization. Private keys for signing CAs are encrypted
with AES-256-GCM and stored separately; non-signing CAs only store
the public certificate.

organization_id.description: The organization this CA belongs to.

subject.description: The certificate subject (e.g., `CN=ACME Corp Root CA`).

Certificate.description:
A tenant-level certificate for users, services, or IoT devices.

Certificates are signed by the organization's CA. The private key is
returned once on generation and never stored by AXIAM.

issuer_ca_id.description: The CA certificate that signed this certificate.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata (e.g., device serial, user ID binding).

tenant_id.description: The tenant this certificate belongs to.

CertificateStatus.description: Status of a certificate in its lifecycle.

CertificateType.description: The purpose for which a certificate was issued.

CheckAccessBody.description: Body for a single authorization check.

resource_id.description: The resource UUID the action targets.

subject_id.description:
Subject to check on behalf of.

When `Some`, the caller must hold `authz:check_as` (T-15-01).
When `None`, the check is performed for the authenticated caller.

CheckAccessResponse.description: Result of a single authorization check.

reason.description: Engine-provided deny reason (T-15-02: generic string only, no resource-structure hints).

CreateCaCertificate.description: Fields required to generate a new CA certificate (user-facing DTO).

CreateCertificate.description: Fields required to generate a new tenant certificate (user-facing DTO).

allowed_algorithms.description:
Accepted JWT signing algorithms (OIDC) or signature algorithms (SAML).
Defaults to `["RS256"]` when not provided (CQ-B40/REQ-14 AC-5).

attribute_map.description: Maps external IdP attributes to AXIAM user fields.

client_id.description: OAuth2 client ID registered with the external IdP.

client_secret.description: OAuth2 client secret registered with the external IdP.

idp_signing_cert_pem.description:
PEM-encoded X.509 certificate for verifying SAML assertions or
OIDC signatures (CQ-B40/REQ-14 AC-5).  Required for SAML configs.

metadata_url.description: OIDC discovery URL or SAML metadata URL.

provider.description: Display name for the identity provider (e.g., "Google", "Okta").

description.description: Description of what this rule monitors.

events.description: Event types that trigger this rule.

grant_types.description: Grant types this client is authorized to use.

redirect_uris.description: Allowed redirect URIs (must be HTTPS, except localhost for dev).

CreateOrganization.description: Fields required to create a new organization.

CreatePgpKey.description: User-facing DTO for generating a new PGP key.

email.description: Email for the OpenPGP User ID.
Email for the OpenPGP User ID.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the account's purpose.
Optional human-readable description of the account's purpose.

CreateTenant.description: Fields required to create a new tenant.

CreateTenantRequest.description: Request body for tenant creation (organization_id comes from the URL path).

password.description: Raw password (will be hashed with Argon2id before storage).

secret.description: HMAC-SHA256 shared secret for signing payloads.

url.description: The HTTPS URL to deliver events to.

DeviceAuthResponse.description: Response returned when a device authenticates via certificate.

EmailConfig.description: Fully resolved email configuration (all fields present).

scope_id.description: The org_id or tenant_id this config belongs to.

EmailConfigOverride.description:
Partial tenant overrides for email configuration.
`None` = inherit from org baseline.

oneOf.description: Full provider replacement (no partial merge within a provider).

EncryptedExport.description: Result of encrypting data with a PGP public key.

private_key_pem.description: PEM-encoded private key — returned only on generation.

GeneratedCaCertificate.description:
Response returned when a CA certificate is generated.

Includes the private key PEM, which is returned **once** and never
stored or retrievable again.

private_key_pem.description: PEM-encoded private key — returned only on generation.

GeneratedCertificate.description:
Response returned when a tenant certificate is generated.

Includes the private key PEM, returned **once** and never stored.

private_key_armored.description:
ASCII-armored private key — returned only on generation for `Export` keys.
For `AuditSigning` keys this is `None` (private key is server-side only).

GeneratedPgpKey.description: Response returned when a PGP key is generated.

Group.description:
A group of users that can access resources based on their roles
and permissions. Groups simplify role management by allowing roles
to be assigned to a group rather than individual users.

KeyAlgorithm.description: The type of key algorithm used for a certificate.

LoginSuccessResponse.description:
Login success response body.

Tokens are delivered via `Set-Cookie` headers — not in this body.

LoginUserInfo.description: User info included in login/me responses.

org_slug.description:
26-05 (D-14): same contract as `tenant_slug`, resolved via the
tenant's `organization_id`.

tenant_id.description:
23-06: exposes the caller's raw tenant_id UUID so the frontend can
carry it into tenant-scoped unauthenticated calls that require it
(e.g. `resendVerification`'s `ResendVerificationRequest.tenant_id`)
without re-deriving it from a slug. Already known server-side from
the validated JWT / AuthenticatedUser extractor — this only exposes
it, it does not widen trust.

tenant_slug.description:
26-05 (D-14): lets the frontend restore the selected tenant after a
hard reload without a redundant lookup. Resolved strictly from the
authenticated user's own `tenant_id` (never request input — T-26-05-01)
and omitted (not an error) when the lookup fails (D-15).

NotificationEventType.description: Events that can trigger an admin notification.

NotificationRule.description: A notification rule that determines which events trigger admin emails.

OAuth2ClientCreatedResponse.description: Response for client creation -- includes the one-time plaintext secret.

config_id.description: ID of the federation config to use.

nonce.description: Nonce for replay protection (generated by the caller).

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI the external IdP should return the user to.

state.description: CSRF state value (generated by the caller).

url.description: The full authorization URL to redirect the user to.

code.description: Authorization code returned by the external IdP.

config_id.description: ID of the federation config used in the authorization request.

nonce.description:
Client-supplied nonce (SECHRD-07/D-04: retained for backward
compatibility with older callers, but IGNORED for verification —
`expected_nonce` always comes from the server-side `FederationLoginState`
row looked up via `state`, never from this field).

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI that was used in the authorization request.

state.description:
CSRF state value from the authorization request. Used to look up the
server-side `FederationLoginState` row that holds the real nonce
(SECHRD-07/D-04) — required so the callback can find its login state.

redirect_uri.description:
SPA post-login destination (stored server-side; returned in callback
response body so the SPA can route after receiving cookies).

OidcStartResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/v1/auth/federation/oidc/start`.
The nonce is intentionally omitted — it stays server-side (T-04-31).

authorize_url.description: The IdP authorization URL to redirect the user to.

state.description: CSRF state value (must be round-tripped through the IdP).

Organization.description:
An organization groups multiple tenants under a single administrative entity.

Organizations represent companies, departments, or business units.
CA certificates are registered at the organization level, enabling
a hierarchical trust model across all tenants.

PermissionGrant.description:
A permission grant with optional scope constraints.
Empty `scope_ids` means the grant covers all scopes (wildcard).

PgpKey.description: An OpenPGP key stored by AXIAM.

PgpKeyPurpose.description: The purpose of an OpenPGP key.

RefreshSuccessResponse.description:
Refresh success response body.

The new access token is delivered via `Set-Cookie` headers.

RequestResetBody.description:
Body for the request-reset endpoint.

`tenant_id` is optional (Open Question 1 / Assumption A4): the public
forgot-password page has no prior tenant context, so it may instead
supply `org_slug`/`tenant_slug` (mirroring `handlers/auth.rs`'s
`(Option<Uuid>, Option<&str>)` login pattern). Slug-resolution failure
is enumeration-safe (D-05) — it funnels into the SAME uniform
`{"sent": true}` response as an unknown account, never a distinct
error status.

parent_id.description: Parent resource ID for hierarchical organization. `None` for root resources.

RetryPolicy.description: Retry policy for failed webhook deliveries.

initial_delay_secs.description: Initial delay between retries in seconds.

is_global.description: Global roles grant permissions across all resources.

RoleAssignment.description: A role together with its assignment context (the resource it is scoped to).

resource_id.description: `None` means the role was assigned globally (no resource scope).
The resource this scope belongs to.

SecuritySettings.description: Fully resolved security settings (all fields present).

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the account's purpose.

ServiceAccountCreatedResponse.description: Response for service account creation — includes the one-time plaintext secret.

SetOrgEmailConfig.description: Input for setting organization-level email config.

SetOrgSettings.description: Input for setting organization-level security settings.

SettingsScope.description: Whether a settings row belongs to an organization or a tenant.

SignAuditBatchRequest.description: Request body for signing an audit batch.

SignedAuditBatch.description: A signed batch of audit log entries.

signature_armored.description: ASCII-armored PGP signed message containing the audit batch payload.

SmtpConfig.description:
SMTP-specific configuration.

`password` is write-only (D-01): `#[serde(skip_serializing)]` means it is
never emitted in a GET/serialized response. On the write path (D-02),
`#[serde(default)]` lets a caller omit the field entirely (deserializing
to `""`); an empty string is the sentinel for "no new secret supplied —
preserve whatever is already stored" (see `SurrealEmailConfigRepository::
set_org_config`). A non-empty value is a real secret to encrypt+replace.

SsoLoginSuccessResponse.description:
Returned by both OIDC callback and SAML ACS on success (alongside
Set-Cookie headers). The `redirect_uri` field allows the SPA to route
the user to the originally requested destination.

redirect_uri.description: SPA destination URL stored during start/login.

Tenant.description:
A tenant is an isolated context within an organization.

Each tenant has its own set of users, roles, permissions, resources,
certificates, and configuration. Tenants can represent environments
(dev/staging/prod) or separate business contexts.

organization_id.description: The organization this tenant belongs to.

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier within the organization (e.g., `production`).

status.description: Lifecycle status. New tenants default to [`TenantStatus::Active`].

TenantSettingsOverride.description: Partial tenant overrides. `None` = inherit from org baseline.

TenantStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a tenant.

A `Suspended` tenant remains stored and its data isolated, but is treated as
administratively disabled. New tenants are `Active` by default.

idp_signing_cert_pem.description:
PEM-encoded X.509 certificate for verifying SAML assertions
(CQ-B40/REQ-14 AC-5).  `Some(None)` clears the stored cert.

UpdateOrganization.description: Fields that can be updated on an existing organization.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata (the admin UI stores `description` here).

UpdateTenant.description: Fields that can be updated on an existing tenant.

oneOf.description: Change the tenant's lifecycle status (e.g. suspend/reactivate).

secret.description:
New HMAC-SHA256 shared secret (D-02 secret rotation). Encrypted
server-side with AES-256-GCM before storage; omit to leave the
existing secret untouched.

deletion_pending.description: GDPR Art. 17 — set when user requests account deletion (D-08).

mfa_secret.description: AES-256-GCM encrypted TOTP secret (if MFA is enrolled).

scheduled_purge_at.description: Scheduled purge date when `deletion_pending` is true (D-08).

totp_last_used_step.description:
The TOTP time-step counter value last successfully verified.

Used to prevent replay attacks: a code whose step ≤ this value is
rejected even if the HMAC is correct (SEC-008).

UserResponse.description: Public-safe user representation (no password_hash, no mfa_secret).

email_verified.description:
True when the user's email address has been verified
(`email_verified_at` is set). Exposed for the admin UI / profile.

failed_login_attempts.description: Number of consecutive failed login attempts.

is_locked.description: Whether the account is currently locked (locked_until is in the future).

locked_until.description: Timestamp until which the account is locked, if any.

WebhookResponse.description: Webhook response — omits the shared secret.
